AC-1.B: Extend MFA enforcement to all users, not just administrators
AC-1.C: Make MFA mandatory for all user accounts immediately
AC-1.D: Implement MFA as highest priority — this is a critical access control gap

AC-2.B: Move to quarterly access reviews to detect privilege creep earlier
AC-2.C: Establish a formal quarterly access review process with documented sign-off
AC-2.D: Implement immediate access review and establish regular review cycle

AC-3.B: Complete least privilege implementation across all user roles
AC-3.C: Prioritise least privilege implementation — start with admin accounts
AC-3.D: Immediately audit and restrict all user access to minimum required

DP-1.B: Document and verify the encryption standard used for compliance evidence
DP-1.C: Extend encryption to all data fields, not just sensitive ones
DP-1.D: Implement AES-256 encryption at rest immediately — critical data protection gap

DP-2.B: Plan migration to TLS 1.3 within next 6 months
DP-2.C: Disable TLS versions below 1.2 immediately and plan TLS 1.3 upgrade
DP-2.D: Implement TLS encryption in transit immediately — critical vulnerability

DP-3.B: Complete data classification across all data types and systems
DP-3.C: Accelerate data classification project — required for GDPR compliance
DP-3.D: Conduct immediate data classification exercise before any further processing

VM-1.B: Establish separate SLA for critical patches — 30 days is too slow for critical CVEs
VM-1.C: Move to monthly patching at minimum — quarterly leaves systems exposed too long
VM-1.D: Establish formal patch management process immediately

VM-2.B: Add continuous vulnerability scanning between annual pentests
VM-2.C: Establish regular scheduled scanning and annual penetration testing
VM-2.D: Commission immediate vulnerability assessment and establish regular testing programme

IR-1.B: Schedule incident response plan test within next 90 days
IR-1.C: Formalise and document the incident response plan and schedule a test
IR-1.D: Develop incident response plan immediately — required for ISO 27001 and DORA compliance

IR-2.B: Extend log retention to minimum 12 months for compliance and forensics
IR-2.C: Implement centralised logging and extend retention period
IR-2.D: Implement centralised logging immediately — essential for incident detection and forensics

CC-1.B: Consider pursuing SOC 2 audit for additional assurance
CC-1.C: Accelerate certification timeline — target ISO 27001 as priority

CC-2.B: Document and remediate identified GDPR gaps with target dates
CC-2.C: Complete GDPR compliance assessment and remediate gaps before processing personal data
CC-2.D: Conduct immediate GDPR assessment — non-compliance carries significant regulatory risk
